BIO: Lord of Egremont, Cumberland.

WILLIAM FitzRanulf, son of RANULF Vicomte du Bessin [Bayeux …

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
William Meschin (sometimes William le Meschin; died between 1130 and 1135) was an Anglo-Norman nobleman and baron. The brother of the earl of Chester, Meschin participated in the First Crusade. After returning to England, he acquired lands both from King Henry I of England and by his marriage to an heiress.

«b»Early life«/b»
Meschin was the brother of Ranulf le Meschin, the Earl of Chester. They were the sons of Ranulf de Briquessart, the viscount of the Bessin, and his wife, Matilda, the daughter of Richard le Goz, Viscount of Avranches. The boys were also nephews of Hugh d'Avranches, who was Earl of Chester. There was also an older brother, Richard, who died young, and a sister, who married Robert de Grandmesnil. Meschin went on the First Crusade and was present at the Siege of Nicaea in 1097.

«b»Acquisition of lands«/b»
Meschin was given Gilsland by his brother, in order to protect the approaches to Carlisle. Due to attacks by the Scots, Meschin was unable to hold it. In compensation, King Henry I of England gave him lands around Allerdale in Cumberland, which centred on Egremont. These lands comprised the lordship, or Barony of Egremont. Through his wife, he acquired Skipton, and the lordship, or Barony of Skipton. Besides these lands, Meschin also was awarded two escheated properties in Leicestershire, some of which had earlier been lands of Roger de Busli. Other properties in Leicestershire were previously held by Durand Malet and William Blund in the Domesday Book. Meschin also held lands in Lincolnshire and Cheshire from his brother.

Meschin built the original parts of Egremont Castle at Egremont. It was a motte-and-bailey stone castle on the River Ehen. He and his wife also founded the Augustinian priory of Embsay Priory. Katrina Legg argues that Meschin's main motive for the foundation was to show support for a monastic order that enjoyed the patronage of King Henry I. Against this, Legg feels that Meschin's wife's motives were more probably religious, as she was close to Thurstan, the Archbishop of York. Another religious foundation of Meschin's was St Bees Priory, a daughter house of St Mary's Abbey, York. St Bees was founded around the same time as Embsay, Embsay was founded between 1120 and 1121, and St Bees was founded sometime after 1120.

Meschin married Cecily, daughter of Robert de Rumily. They had one son, Ranulf Meschin, and three daughters, Alice, Avice, and Matilda. Another son, Matthew, who was the eldest son, died before his parents.

«b»Death and legacy«/b»
Meschin died between 1130 and 1135. His widow married Henry de Tracy, and she died around 1151. Skipton went to Alice, who married William fitzDuncan. Alice and her first husband also acquired Egremont, after the death of her brother Ranulf without heirs. After the death of fitzDuncan, Alice married Alexander fitzGerold. Avice married William de Courcy. Avice married as her second husband William Paynel, son of Ralph Paynel, as his second wife. Before 1153 Avice had married as her third husband Walter, the son of Alan de Percy. Matilda married twice, first to Philip de Belmeis and second to Hugh de Mortimer. All three daughters were the eventual co-heirs of their father, after their brother died without offspring between 1135 and 1140. Meschin's lands were divided between their descendants, with parts ending up with the Counts of Aumale, the family of the Courcys, and the last and smallest part to the Mortimer family.

zh.lifeSummary: 當William le Meschin於大約1078年出生在法國諾門迪時，他的父親Ranulf de Briquessart33歲，母親Marguerite d'Avranches25歲。他於大約1110年與Cecily Rumilly結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他於1097年登記服兵役。他去世於1135年在英國波藍德享年58歲，埋葬在法國帕-德-卡拉斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William le Meschin于大约1078年出生在法国諾門迪时，他的父亲Ranulf de Briquessart33岁，母亲Marguerite d'Avranches25岁。他于大约1110年与Cecily Rumilly结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他于1097年登记服兵役。他去世于1135年在英国波藍德享年58岁，埋葬在法国帕-德-卡拉斯。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Meschin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Meacham
meanings: English: variant of Machen . It may occasionally also be a variant of Measham, a habitational name from Measham in Leicestershire. The place takes its name from the river Mease (from Old English mēos ‘marsh, bog’) + Old English hām ‘village, homestead’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When William le Meschin was born about 1078, in Normandy, France, his father, Ranulf de Briquessart, was 33 and his mother, Marguerite d'Avranches, was 25. He married Cecily Rumilly about 1110. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. He registered for military service in 1097. He died in 1135, in Cumberland, England, at the age of 58, and was buried in Pas-de-Calais, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William le Meschin est né environ 1078, à Normandie, France, son père, Ranulf de Briquessart, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Marguerite d'Avranches, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Cecily Rumilly environ 1110. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1097. Il est décédé en 1135, à Cumberland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 58 ans, et a été enterré à Pas-de-Calais, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als William le Meschin um 1078 in Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Ranulf de Briquessart 33 und seine Mutter Marguerite d'Avranches 25. Er hat um 1110 Cecily Rumilly geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Er hat sich 1097 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb 1135 im Alter von 58 Jahren in Cumberland, England und wurde in Pas-de-Calais, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William le Meschin è nato il circa 1078 nel Normandie, Francia, il suo padre, Ranulf de Briquessart, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Marguerite d'Avranches, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Cecily Rumilly circa 1110. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1097. Morì nel 1135, nel Cumberland, England, all'età di 58 anni, e fu sepolto a Pas-de-Calais, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: William le Meschinが約1078年にフランスノーマンディで生まれた時，父Ranulf de Briquessartは33歳，母Marguerite d'Avranchesは25歳であった。約1110年にCecily Rumillyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1097年に兵役登録をした。彼は1135年にイギリスクウンバーランドで58歳で死去し，フランスプアス-デ-カレイスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William le Meschin(이)가 대략 1078년 프랑스 노먼디에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ranulf de Briquessart(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Marguerite d'Avranches(은)는 25세였다. 그는 대략 1110년 Cecily Rumilly(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1097년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1135년 영국 쿠벌랜드에서 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 파스-데-캘레이에 묻혔다.

mn.Egremont, Cumberland, England: Egremont, Cumbraland, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William le Meschin nasceu aproximadamente 1078, em Normandie, França, seu pai, Ranulf de Briquessart, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Marguerite d'Avranches, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Cecily Rumilly aproximadamente 1110. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1097. Ele faleceu em 1135, em Cumberland, Inglaterra, com 58 anos, e foi sepultado em Pas-de-Calais, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William le Meschin родился около 1078 г. в местности норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени Ranulf de Briquessart было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Marguerite d'Avranches, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Cecily Rumilly около 1110 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. В 1097 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер в 1135 г. в местности кумбирланд, Англия в возрасте 58 лет и был похоронен в местности пас-де-калеи, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William le Meschin nació aproximadamente 1078, en Normandie, Francia, su papá, Ranulf de Briquessart, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Marguerite d'Avranches, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Cecily Rumilly aproximadamente 1110. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1097. Murió en 1135, en Cumberland, Inglaterra, a la edad de 58 años, y fue sepultado en Pas-de-Calais, Francia.
